NAME

common.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void build_ramp64 (uint8_t ramp64[RAMP64_SIZE], const char *ascii_chars)
double calculate_cache_eviction_score (uint64_t last_access_time, uint32_t access_count, uint64_t creation_time, uint64_t current_time)
utf8_palette_cache_t * get_utf8_palette_cache (const char *ascii_chars)
void build_utf8_luminance_cache (const char *ascii_chars, utf8_char_t cache[256])
void build_utf8_ramp64_cache (const char *ascii_chars, utf8_char_t cache64[64], uint8_t char_index_ramp[256])
void simd_caches_destroy_all (void)
void init_dec3 (void)
Initialize decimal-to-ASCII cache (0-255 → 0"to255). void init_default_luminance_palette (void)
void ascii_simd_init (void)
Initialize SIMD subsystem.

Variables


global_dec3_cache_t g_dec3_cache = {0}
char g_default_luminance_palette [256] = {0}

Function Documentation

void ascii_simd_init (void )

Initialize SIMD subsystem. Initializes all SIMD subsystems and caches. Must be called once at startup.

610   init_dec3();

void
  build_ramp64 (uint8_t ramp64[RAMP64_SIZE], const char * ascii_chars)


Definition at line 41 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


41                                                                         {
42   if (!ascii_chars) {
43     // Fallback to space character
44     for (int i = 0; i < RAMP64_SIZE; i++) {
45       ramp64[i] = ' ';
46     }
47     return;
48   }
49 
50   // Use UTF-8 palette functions for proper character handling
51   utf8_palette_t *utf8_pal = utf8_palette_create(ascii_chars);
52   if (!utf8_pal) {
53     // Fallback to space character
54     for (int i = 0; i < RAMP64_SIZE; i++) {
55       ramp64[i] = ' ';
56     }
57     return;
58   }
59 
60   size_t char_count = utf8_palette_get_char_count(utf8_pal);
61   if (char_count == 0) {
62     // No valid characters found, use space
63     for (int i = 0; i < RAMP64_SIZE; i++) {
64       ramp64[i] = ' ';
65     }
66     utf8_palette_destroy(utf8_pal);
67     return;
68   }
69 
70   // Build the ramp64 lookup using UTF-8 character indices
71   for (int i = 0; i < RAMP64_SIZE; i++) {
72     // Map 0-63 to 0-(char_count-1) using proper character indexing
73     size_t char_idx = (i * (char_count - 1) + (RAMP64_SIZE - 1) / 2) / (RAMP64_SIZE - 1);
74     if (char_idx >= char_count) {
75       char_idx = char_count - 1;
76     }
77 
78     // Get the first byte of the character at this character index
79     const utf8_char_info_t *char_info = utf8_palette_get_char(utf8_pal, char_idx);
80     if (char_info) {
81       ramp64[i] = (uint8_t)char_info->bytes[0];
82     } else {
83       ramp64[i] = ' '; // Fallback
84     }
85   }
86 
87   utf8_palette_destroy(utf8_pal);
88 }






References utf8_palette_create(),
    utf8_palette_destroy(), utf8_palette_get_char(), and
    utf8_palette_get_char_count().






void
  build_utf8_luminance_cache (const char * ascii_chars, utf8_char_t
  cache[256])


Definition at line 387 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


387                                                                                  {
388   if (!ascii_chars || !cache)
389     return;
390 
391   // Parse characters
392   typedef struct {
393     const char *start;
394     int byte_len;
395   } char_info_t;
396 
397   char_info_t char_infos[256];
398   int char_count = 0;
399   const char *p = ascii_chars;
400 
401   while (*p && char_count < 255) {
402     char_infos[char_count].start = p;
403 
404     if ((*p & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
405       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 2;
406       p += 2;
407     } else if ((*p & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
408       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 3;
409       p += 3;
410     } else if ((*p & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
411       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 4;
412       p += 4;
413     } else {
414       // ASCII characters (0x00-0x7F) and invalid sequences: treat as single byte
415       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 1;
416       p++;
417     }
418     char_count++;
419   }
420 
421   // Handle empty string case
422   if (char_count == 0)
423     return;
424 
425   // Build 256-entry cache
426   for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
427     int char_idx = char_count > 1 ? (i * (char_count - 1) + 127) / 255 : 0;
428     if (char_idx >= char_count)
429       char_idx = char_count - 1;
430 
431     cache[i].byte_len = char_infos[char_idx].byte_len;
432     memcpy(cache[i].utf8_bytes, char_infos[char_idx].start, char_infos[char_idx].byte_len);
433     if (cache[i].byte_len < 4) {
434       cache[i].utf8_bytes[cache[i].byte_len] = '\0';
435     }
436   }
437 }






Referenced by get_utf8_palette_cache().






void
  build_utf8_ramp64_cache (const char * ascii_chars, utf8_char_t cache64[64],
  uint8_t char_index_ramp[256])


Definition at line 440 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


440                                                                                                              {
441   if (!ascii_chars || !cache64 || !char_index_ramp)
442     return;
443 
444   // Reuse the luminance cache building logic but for 64 entries
445   // (Same UTF-8 parsing as above, but map to 64 entries instead of 256)
446 
447   // Parse characters (same as above)
448   typedef struct {
449     const char *start;
450     int byte_len;
451   } char_info_t;
452 
453   char_info_t char_infos[256];
454   int char_count = 0;
455   const char *p = ascii_chars;
456 
457   while (*p && char_count < 255) {
458     char_infos[char_count].start = p;
459 
460     if ((*p & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
461       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 2;
462       p += 2;
463     } else if ((*p & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
464       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 3;
465       p += 3;
466     } else if ((*p & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
467       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 4;
468       p += 4;
469     } else {
470       // ASCII characters (0x00-0x7F) and invalid sequences: treat as single byte
471       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 1;
472       p++;
473     }
474     char_count++;
475   }
476 
477   // Handle empty string case
478   if (char_count == 0)
479     return;
480 
481   // Build 64-entry cache and index ramp
482   for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) {
483     int char_idx = char_count > 1 ? (i * (char_count - 1) + 31) / 63 : 0;
484     if (char_idx >= char_count)
485       char_idx = char_count - 1;
486 
487     // Store character index for SIMD lookup
488     char_index_ramp[i] = (uint8_t)char_idx;
489 
490     // Cache UTF-8 character
491     cache64[i].byte_len = char_infos[char_idx].byte_len;
492     memcpy(cache64[i].utf8_bytes, char_infos[char_idx].start, char_infos[char_idx].byte_len);
493     if (cache64[i].byte_len < 4) {
494       cache64[i].utf8_bytes[cache64[i].byte_len] = '\0';
495     }
496   }
497 }






Referenced by get_utf8_palette_cache().






double
  calculate_cache_eviction_score (uint64_t last_access_time, uint32_t
  access_count, uint64_t creation_time, uint64_t current_time)


Definition at line 91 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


92                                                              {
93   // Protect against unsigned underflow if times are inconsistent (clock adjustments, etc.)
94   uint64_t age_ns = (current_time >= last_access_time) ? (current_time - last_access_time) : 0;
95   uint64_t total_age_ns = (current_time >= creation_time) ? (current_time - creation_time) : 0;
96 
97   uint64_t age_seconds = age_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
98   uint64_t total_age_seconds = total_age_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
99 
100   // Frequency factor: high-use palettes get protection (logarithmic scaling)
101   double frequency_factor = 1.0 + log10(1.0 + access_count);
102 
103   // Aging factor: frequency bonus decays over time (5-minute half-life)
104   double aging_factor = exp(-(double)age_seconds / CACHE_FREQUENCY_DECAY_TIME);
105 
106   // Recent access bonus: strong protection for recently used (1-minute protection)
107   double recency_bonus = exp(-(double)age_seconds / CACHE_RECENCY_SCALE);
108 
109   // Cache lifetime penalty: prevent immortal caches (1-hour max lifetime)
110   double lifetime_penalty = total_age_seconds > CACHE_MAX_LIFETIME ? 0.5 : 1.0;
111 
112   // Final score: higher = keep longer
113   return (frequency_factor * aging_factor + recency_bonus) * lifetime_penalty;
114 }






Referenced by get_utf8_palette_cache().






utf8_palette_cache_t
  * get_utf8_palette_cache (const char * ascii_chars)


Definition at line 278 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


278                                                                       {
279   if (!ascii_chars)
280     return NULL;
281 
282   // Check for empty string
283   if (ascii_chars[0] == '\0')
284     return NULL;
285 
286   // Create hash of palette for cache key
287   uint32_t palette_hash = hash_palette_string(ascii_chars);
288 
289   // Ensure the cache system is initialized
290   init_utf8_cache_system();
291 
292   // First try: read lock for cache lookup (allows multiple concurrent readers)
293   rwlock_rdlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
294 
295   // Check if cache exists
296   utf8_palette_cache_t *cache = NULL;
297   HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &palette_hash, cache);
298   if (cache) {
299     // Cache hit: Update access tracking (atomics are thread-safe under rdlock)
300     uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
301     atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
302     uint32_t new_access_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&cache->access_count, 1) + 1;
303 
304     // Every 10th access: Update heap position (requires write lock)
305     if (new_access_count % 10 == 0) {
306       // Save the key before releasing read lock
307       uint32_t saved_key = cache->key;
308 
309       // Release read lock and upgrade to write lock
310       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
311       rwlock_wrlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
312 
313       // Re-lookup cache entry after lock upgrade
314       // Another thread could have evicted this entry while we were upgrading locks
315       utf8_palette_cache_t *cache_relookup = NULL;
316       HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &saved_key, cache_relookup);
317       if (cache_relookup) {
318         // Cache entry still exists - safe to update heap position
319         uint64_t last_access = atomic_load_u64(&cache_relookup->last_access_time);
320         uint32_t access_count = atomic_load_u64(&cache_relookup->access_count);
321         double new_score =
322             calculate_cache_eviction_score(last_access, access_count, cache_relookup->creation_time, current_time);
323         utf8_heap_update_score(cache_relookup, new_score);
324       }
325       // If cache_relookup is NULL, entry was evicted - skip update (not an error)
326 
327       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
328     } else {
329       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
330     }
331 
332     return cache;
333   }
334 
335   // Cache miss: Need to create new entry
336   // Release read lock and acquire write lock
337   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
338   rwlock_wrlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
339 
340   // Double-check: another thread might have created it while we upgraded locks
341   cache = NULL;
342   HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &palette_hash, cache);
343   if (cache) {
344     // Found it! Just update access tracking and return
345     uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
346     atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
347     atomic_fetch_add_u64(&cache->access_count, 1);
348     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
349     return cache;
350   }
351 
352   // Create new cache entry (holding write lock)
353   cache = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(utf8_palette_cache_t), utf8_palette_cache_t *);
354   if (!cache) {
355     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
356     return NULL;
357   }
358   memset(cache, 0, sizeof(utf8_palette_cache_t));
359 
360   // Set the key for uthash
361   cache->key = palette_hash;
362 
363   // Build both cache types
364   build_utf8_luminance_cache(ascii_chars, cache->cache);
365   build_utf8_ramp64_cache(ascii_chars, cache->cache64, cache->char_index_ramp);
366 
367   // Store palette hash for validation
368   SAFE_STRNCPY(cache->palette_hash, ascii_chars, sizeof(cache->palette_hash));
369   cache->is_valid = true;
370 
371   // Initialize eviction tracking
372   uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
373   atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
374   atomic_store_u64(&cache->access_count, 1); // First access
375   cache->creation_time = current_time;
376 
377   // Store in hash table with guaranteed eviction support
378   try_insert_with_eviction_utf8(palette_hash, cache);
379 
380   log_debug("UTF8_CACHE: Created new cache for palette='%s' (hash=0x%x)", ascii_chars, palette_hash);
381 
382   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
383   return cache;
384 }






References atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    build_utf8_luminance_cache(), build_utf8_ramp64_cache(),
    calculate_cache_eviction_score(), SAFE_MALLOC, and
    time_get_ns().


Referenced by image_print(), image_print_16color(),
    image_print_16color_dithered(),
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(),
    image_print_color(), and session_display_create().






void
  init_dec3 (void )


Initialize decimal-to-ASCII cache (0-255 → "0" to
    "255"). Pre-builds a cache of ASCII representations for all byte
    values. Used by color code generation to avoid repeated int-to-string
    conversions.


Definition at line 554 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


554                      {
555   if (g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized)
556     return;
557   for (int v = 0; v < 256; ++v) {
558     int d2 = v / 100;     // 0..2
559     int r = v - d2 * 100; // 0..99
560     int d1 = r / 10;      // 0..9
561     int d0 = r - d1 * 10; // 0..9
562 
563     if (d2) {
564       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].len = 3;
565       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[0] = '0' + d2;
566       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[1] = '0' + d1;
567       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[2] = '0' + d0;
568     } else if (d1) {
569       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].len = 2;
570       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[0] = '0' + d1;
571       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[1] = '0' + d0;
572     } else {
573       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].len = 1;
574       g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[v].s[0] = '0' + d0;
575     }
576   }
577   g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized = true;
578 }






References g_dec3_cache.


Referenced by append_truecolor_bg(),
    append_truecolor_fg(), append_truecolor_fg_bg(), and
    ascii_simd_init().






void
  init_default_luminance_palette (void )


Definition at line 597 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


597                                           {
598   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_default_palette_lc, "default_palette")) {
599     return; // Already initialized
600   }
601   do_init_default_luminance_palette();
602 }






References lifecycle_init().


Referenced by ascii_simd_init().






void
  simd_caches_destroy_all (void )


Definition at line 504 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


504                                    {
505   log_dev("SIMD_CACHE: Starting cleanup of all SIMD caches");
506 
507   // Only destroy caches if they were ever initialized
508   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_utf8_cache_lc)) {
509     // Destroy shared UTF-8 palette cache (write lock for cleanup)
510     rwlock_wrlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
511     if (g_utf8_cache_table) {
512       // Free all UTF-8 cache entries using HASH_ITER
513       utf8_palette_cache_t *cache, *tmp;
514       HASH_ITER(hh, g_utf8_cache_table, cache, tmp) {
515         HASH_DEL(g_utf8_cache_table, cache);
516         SAFE_FREE(cache);
517       }
518       g_utf8_cache_table = NULL;
519       log_debug("UTF8_CACHE: Destroyed shared UTF-8 palette cache");
520     }
521     // Clean up heap arrays
522     if (g_utf8_heap) {
523       SAFE_FREE(g_utf8_heap);
524       g_utf8_heap = NULL;
525       g_utf8_heap_size = 0;
526     }
527     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
528     // Shutdown lifecycle and destroy rwlock together
529     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_utf8_cache_lc);
530   }
531 
532   // Call architecture-specific cache cleanup functions
533   // Note: Only ONE SIMD implementation is compiled based on highest available instruction set
534   // Higher instruction sets (AVX2, SSSE3) handle cleanup for lower ones (SSE2)
535 #if SIMD_SUPPORT_NEON
536   neon_caches_destroy();
537 #elif SIMD_SUPPORT_SSSE3
538   ssse3_caches_destroy();
539 #elif SIMD_SUPPORT_SSE2
540   sse2_caches_destroy();
541 #elif SIMD_SUPPORT_SVE
542   sve_caches_destroy();
543 #endif
544 
545   log_dev("SIMD_CACHE: All SIMD caches destroyed");
546 }






References lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), and SAFE_FREE.
